danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Obstacles and traps within large environments
danger_signals.meaning: Navigational danger requiring platforming skill or alternative routes

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctly designed enemy characters at level transitions
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring strategy change and sustained engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable items scattered throughout environments (grappling gun, ammunition)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability for progression and survival
Mobility and tactical options for evasion or approach

safety_signals.visual_form: Player-controlled character with distinctive silhouette and cape
safety_signals.meaning: Agency and control over progression through world

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Batcave areas (in MS-DOS version context), evidence of criminal investigation

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Thugs and henchmen with distinct combat styles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Industrial/underworld settings associated with Penguin's criminal operations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy sprites positioned in player's path with aggressive stances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies attack, Batman must fight or evade; screen space becomes contested

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large open environments rendered with platform layouts, obstacles, collectibles visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Batman moves freely, can collect power-ups, search for progression routes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Few or no visible power-ups in environment; Batman's visual state may show wear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must exercise caution, manage limited ammunition or equipment efficiently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large-scale enemy character with distinct visual design, elevated arena or contained space
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat becomes pattern-based; boss exhibits telegraphed attack patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through distinct environments associated with antagonists (Penguin's territory, Catwoman's domain) communicates character threat and story advancement
Grappling gun availability in specific locations signals intended exploration paths and mechanical expansion
Different thug designs within same faction indicate organizational hierarchy and territorial control
Film-referenced cutscenes (in Sega CD version) contextualize combat within movie narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Stationary or predictable patrol patterns; lower threat visibility
Aggressive movement toward Batman; attack initiation
Mobile threat in rooftop/shadow spaces; can appear unpredictably
Restricted movement options; reliance on melee combat or evasion
Access to grappling gun and power-ups enables new navigation paths and tactical options

notes: Batman Returns (Genesis) uses action-adventure design with large environments and power-up gating to communicate progression and threat. The game's visual communication relies on enemy placement, environmental hazard visibility, and power-up scarcity to signal world-state. Faction identity is conveyed through character association (Penguin vs. Catwoman territory) rather than explicit color-coding. The use of collectable resources (grappling gun, ammunition) creates visible safety signals; their absence increases perceived danger. The film license provides narrative context for enemy types and environmental theming.
sources_used: Wikipedia: List of video games featuring Batman
